raw_consumer_statement: I have a Costco credit card that I used primarily to buy from Costco and once in a while shop in other places. In XXXX I noticed a couple of fraudulent charges that summed up to {$860.00}. I called and disputed the charges right away. Few months later Costco sent me a letter that they closed the dispute and can not credit my account for the fraudulent charges because " The card 's XXXX chip was used to make the disputed transactions, so they don't consider the charges fraudulent activity. '' I have explained to them multiple times that I do not make purchases with the type of merchants and do not buy the type of products. Yes, my card is physically with me XXXX. I did not make the purchase and have never entered the stores where the purchases where made in XXXX.
